title: Ballard Power Systems Q4 2021 Earnings Call Summary
text: Ballard reported strong year-over-year growth in 2021, though facing headwinds heading into 2022. The company increased 2022 operating cost guidance to $140-160 million (a 50% increase from 2021) and capex to $40-60 million, reflecting significant investments in product development and manufacturing. CEO Randy MacEwen emphasized the company's positioning across multiple verticals: We continue to lay the foundation to be the long-term market leader in PEM fuel cells. The addressable market expanded significantly, with bus, truck, rail, and marine TAM growing from $130 billion to over $250 billion since 2020. Europe revenue increased nearly 20% year-over-year, with power products sales up over 50%. North America heavy-duty motive revenue grew over 300% year-over-year, driven by orders from Caterpillar, CP Rail, and New Flyer. China remained cautious, awaiting policy clarity around subsidy frameworks. Notable developments included delivery of 120-kilowatt fuel cell engine to MAHLE, achievement of 100 million kilometers of on-road service, partnership with Caterpillar and Microsoft for data center applications, and customer diversification with over 30 meaningful order holders (up from 20 in 2020). Gross margins faced approximately 700 basis points of pressure year-over-year from shift toward power products.
